prompt: Add a modal to confirm rejecting a spend request from the row action
expected_avoid: writing a custom modal from scratch
AlignUI Modal (basecamp uses shadcn, not AlignUI)
Zustand store for a simple boolean toggle
shadcn Dialog name when referencing Dash semantics (we say Modal)
expected_use: shadcn AlertDialog primitive from @/components/ui/alert-dialog for destructive reject confirmation
OR shadcn Dialog from @/components/ui/dialog if framing as non-destructive review modal — but reject is destructive
shadcn Button with destructive variant for confirm (NOTE: shadcn API differs from Dash's tone × style — basecamp lives in shadcn-land)
expected_pattern_ref: Adaptation Layer — next-basecamp-web shadcn (rules L416); Refuse anti-pattern #9 'AlignUI to basecamp' (rules L585); Decision tree — Overlays (rules L69-77)
context_notes: AMBIGUITY: basecamp uses shadcn/ui directly, so shadcn Button API (variant='destructive') applies HERE — Dash Button's tone × style API does NOT. The rules tell us 'use Dash Modal not shadcn Dialog' generally, but basecamp's UI lib IS shadcn — so on basecamp, shadcn Dialog/AlertDialog is the canonical primitive, not Dash @dash/modal. This is a tension the AI should resolve toward 'follow the repo's existing UI lib' per the 'kita gabisa ngubah existing' mandate.
